summary: P1 refactor regression pass. Backend: 9/9 new endpoint-shape tests for the refactored helpers pass (ops/health, withdraw/cron, test-connection dispatch, transactions/export) and the previous P0 suite still passes (15 passed, 1 skipped — same prod-shape forgot-password skip as iteration_1). Frontend: Operator Console renders Total Users / Paid / Messages / Revenue tiles; Users tab is default with per-row +100 / Pause / Delete / Reset 2FA visible; Projects, Money, and Ops tabs all open with their key sub-sections (Stripe / PayPal / NOWPayments cards, Recent payments table, Auto-withdraw, Withdrawal history, Run health check / Run code review / Restart / Deploy). Dashboard sidebar (New session, refer banner, ENTERPRISE plan, Operator console link, Sign out, Sign out everywhere) renders correctly. No console errors beyond a single benign /auth/me 401 probe before the cookie is set. No regressions detected from the pure decomposition refactor.
critical_code_review_comments: autowithdraw_ext.run_auto_withdraw_once iterates `for sweep in (_sweep_stripe(...), _sweep_nowpayments(...)):` then awaits each row. This works but creates the second coroutine before the first is awaited; if you ever decide to parallelise these, switch to asyncio.gather(_sweep_stripe(s), _sweep_nowpayments(s)) — both helpers are independent IO.

payments_ext._CONNECTION_TESTERS dispatch is clean. Suggest adding a runtime assertion (or unit test) that the dispatch table keys match the documented supported set, so future providers aren't silently 400-ing.

ops_ext._check_services treats any non-RUNNING non-core service as ok=True but tags 'non-critical' in detail. Operator console grid may show all-green even when a sidecar (e.g. trial-emails worker) is stopped. Consider surfacing a 'warn' state distinct from ok=True/false to avoid silent regressions.

_parse_export_date_range swallows the original parse exception with a generic 400. Including the offending date in the detail (e.g. 'Invalid date "foo"; use YYYY-MM-DD') would speed up triage with no security impact.

OpsTab orchestrator no longer renders the literal 'Quick actions' label — the three neon-indigo buttons render in OpsQuickActions but the section heading was dropped during the refactor. Cosmetic; not a behavioural regression.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (operator console + dashboard render, no JS errors)

test_credentials: Operator rac.investments.swe@gmail.com / REDACTED. Operator boots with RESET_OPERATOR_PASSWORD=true, so login lands on /setup-2fa; navigate directly to /operator or /dashboard afterwards.
seed_data_creation: None — black-box only. Tests reuse the operator account; transactions/export PDF path is exercised against existing paid txs in the DB (1 paid tx present).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Regression suite now lives in two files: /app/backend/tests/test_p0_review.py (cookie/2FA/codes-file) and /app/backend/tests/test_p1_refactor.py (extracted helpers: ops/health, withdraw/cron, test-connection dispatch, transactions/export). Run the whole folder with `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v`. Note the health-check key is `payments.master_switch` (not `master_payments`). The operator must be 2FA-cleared (RESET_OPERATOR_PASSWORD=true) for the operator-only tests to skip-vs-pass meaningfully.
rca of the issue: N/A — refactor verified clean. Initial test failure on missing `master_payments` key was a test-only assumption mismatch (actual key emitted by _check_master_payments is `payments.master_switch`); fixed in the test file, not in product code.
